_source: Donald W. Engels, Alexander the Great and the Logistics of the Macedonian Army (Univ. of California Press, 1978)
_provenance: Figures recovered from the book's narrative and footnotes. Appendix 5 'Statistical Tables' (Tables 1-8) and the inline 'see table 7' references did NOT survive EPUB/PDF extraction; all values below come from prose, not the master tables. prov tags: engels-stated = a figure Engels adopts; engels-derived = a figure Engels calculates/concludes. See ../PROVENANCE.md.
_units: weight = pounds (lb); water = Imperial gallons (1 Imp gal = 10 lb); distance = miles; time = days. cite = line number(s) in the source content.md.

soldier.note: 2 qt/day water floor cannot be cut for untrained troops in desert

cavalry_horse.note: water swings 5-15 gal with heat; 8 is the working average

pack_mule.note: pack animal rationed as a cavalry horse; a minimum, not reducible

camel.note: grain/straw trade off: 0 grain -> 70 lb straw; water-endurance is overstated

ox_team.note: per 2-ox cart team; oxen banned by Alexander (too slow), given for comparison only

soldier.note: ~80 lb total incl. panoply; ~30 lb can be food

average_pack_animal.note: modeling unit; eats 10 grain + 10 forage of its own load per day

two_ox_cart_pull.note: 1000-1200 lb pull but 2 mph / 5 hr-day; cannot hold the march rate; banned

long_haul_average.note: over long distances incl. mandatory rest days

planning_rate.note: daily marching rate with 1 day's halt in 7

short_distance.note: when long halts need not be made

whole_army_max.note: absolute ceiling, Sinai; achieved ONLY because the fleet carried supplies

small_light_unit.note: 40-50 mpd; detached light units only, never the whole army

infantry_walk_mph.note: 2.5-3 mph on the move; NOT a daily rate

self_carry_grain_days.note: water+forage available; only grain carried. Diverges at N=25 (animal eats its 250-lb load at 10 lb grain/day)

self_carry_grain_forage_days.note: water available, no grazing; diverges at N=12.5 (eats 20 lb/day)

full_desert_days.note: no grain, forage, or water available - the central result of the book

max_waterless_interval_days.note: no caravan account exceeds 4 waterless days

economic_resupply_radius_miles.note: beyond this an overland convoy eats more than it delivers (net-zero at ~75 mi)

rest_day_every_n_days.note: animals cannot bear loads >5-7 consecutive days

max_pack_animals_fielded.note: more would make the column longer than a day's march

consumption_to_carry_ratio.note: constant regardless of animal size; sets the desert range limit

model_constants._note: advise.py uses these to reproduce Engels's self-carry explosion: animals_needed = grain_per_day * N / (250 - 10*N) for grain-only terrain (reproduces his 1,121 / 40,350 / 107,600 figures).

unit_conversions: 1 choinix grain = 1.92 pints = 1.5 lb (Engels works in choinices + lb, not medimnus/artaba)
1 stade ~= 600 ft (Engels finds no evidence for a short Macedonian stade); ~8.7 stades/mile
1 talent ~= 80 lb; largest 5th-c. merchant ship ~= 10,000 talents ~= 400 tons
1 Imperial gallon water = 10 lb = 1.20 US gallons; desert minimum 2 Imp qt/day = 0.5 Imp gal
